title: SystemVerilog unsized literal '1 incorrectly evaluated as 1-bit value instead of filling all bits
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.58+84 (git sha1 f5c9e122d, g++ 15.2.1 -march=x86-64 -mtune=generic -O2 -fno-plt -fexceptions -fstack-clash-protection -fcf-protection -fno-omit-frame-pointer -mno-omit-leaf-frame-pointer -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

```sv
read_verilog -sv <<EOF
module top (
    output logic [3:0] o
);
   localparam logic [3:0] L1 = '1;
   assign o = L1;
   always_comb assert(o == 4'b1111);
endmodule
EOF

prep -top top
chformal -lower
select top
sat -prove-asserts -verify
```


### Expected Behavior

From what I understand of the IEEE-1800-2023 paragraph 5.7.1 Integer literal constants

> An unsized unsigned single-bit value can be specified by preceding the single-bit value with an apostrophe
( ' ), but without the base specifier. All bits of the unsized value shall be set to the value of the specified bit. 
> In a self-determined context, it shall have a width of 1 bit.
> '0, '1, 'X, 'x, 'Z, 'z // sets all bits to specified value

Then the check should succeed, as o should take the value 'b1111

### Actual Behavior

Instead it takes the value 'b0001
